channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: After the deluge, the lies: Misinformation and hoaxes about Helene cloud the recovery
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The facts emerging from Hurricane Helene's destruction are heartrending: Businesses and homes destroyed, whole communities nearly wiped out, hundreds of lives lost, hundreds of people missing.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Inside a North Carolina mountain town that Hurricane Helene nearly wiped off the map
item.description: Nearly 400 miles from where Hurricane Helene made landfall, the North Carolina mountain hamlet of Chimney Rock Village has been all but wiped off the map.
item.dc:creator: Allen G. Breed, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Does a cumulus cloud really weigh a million pounds?
item.description: Just because something floats doesn't mean it is weightless. A great example is when oil is poured onto water. The oil will float on top of the water, but that doesn't mean it has no weight.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Bacca, KRON4

item.title: Russian prosecutors seek 7-year sentence for US man accused of fighting for Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian prosecutors asked for a seven-year sentence in the trial of a U.S. citizen accused of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine against Russia, Russian news agencies reported Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bands drop out of Orlando music festival after Kyle Rittenhouse booked as guest
item.description: Shell Shock organizers describe the festival as a concert charity event that seeks to raise funds for a charity that "supports first responders struggling with injuries to include Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD).
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KRON4

item.title: Judge denies an order sought by a Black student who was punished over his hair
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday denied a request by a Black high school student in Texas for a court order that the student’s lawyers say would have allowed him to return to his high school without fear of having his previous punishment over his hairstyle resume.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Several bands drop out of Orlando heavy metal festival after organizers book Kyle Rittenhouse as guest
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. — An upcoming heavy metal festival set to take place in Florida this month is undergoing some major lineup changes after several bands that were set to perform at the event dropped out after learning that Kyle Rittenhouse had been invited to participate.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WGN 9

item.title: Why are there still so many blackouts after major storms?
item.description: The high costs of infrastructure improvements likely have contributed to the blackouts. Experts say more can be done to prevent power outages, but it would require significant investments.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McDonald's adding Chicken Big Mac to US menus for a limited time
item.description: McDonald's is set to introduce the Chicken Big Mac, a reimagined version of its classic Big Mac, featuring two tempura-battered chicken patties on a sesame seed bun, on Oct. 10.
item.dc:creator: Nick Gladney, WGN 9

item.title: Oklahoma defends Bibles-in-schools proposal after report that only Trump's might qualify
item.description: Oklahoma Education Superintendent Ryan Walters’s (R) office is defending the process for how the state will be selecting newly mandated Bibles in classrooms after a report was released that the criteria is so narrow that essentially no Bibles qualify — besides ones endorsed by former President Trump
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Boris Johnson alleges Netanyahu may have bugged his bathroom
item.description: Israel also was accused around the same time of planting listening devices in The White House, The Telegraph also reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Mayorkas' claim that FEMA is 'tremendously prepared' comes back to haunt him amid Helene aftermath
item.description: Parts of North Carolina and South Carolina have been completely submerged by flooding and communities nearly wiped out by Hurricane Helene, with residents waiting for FEMA help days later.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico’s new president promises to resume fight against climate change
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — In her first days as Mexico’s new president, Claudia Sheinbaum made a point of distancing herself from the fossil fuel reliance promoted by her predecessor and mentor, Andrés Manuel López Obrador, and vowed to resume an energy transition that he halted.
item.dc:creator: TERESA DE MIGUEL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Memphis officers convicted in Tyre Nichols' fatal beating
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Three former Memphis police officers were convicted Thursday in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but were acquitted of the harshest charges they faced for a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Springsteen calls Trump 'most dangerous candidate for president in my lifetime,' backs Harris
item.description: The rock legend said the former president “doesn’t understand the meaning of this country, its history or what it means to be deeply American.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother are youngest-known Helene victims
item.description: A large tree had crashed through the roof, crushing Kobe Williams and causing her to fall on top of infant sons Khyzier and Khazmir. All three were found dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Penn. Republican Senate candidate David McCormick: 'I'm the underdog'
item.description: “I'm the underdog,” David McCormick told NewsNation’s Leland Vittert in a one-on-one interview for “On Balance.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Earth's 2nd moon has arrived: What you need to know
item.description: A second moon has officially entered Earth's orbit—sort of. Although it's being called a "mini-moon," it's actually an asteroid named 2024 PT5. The asteroid has been temporarily captured by Earth's gravitational pull. The asteroid will remain in Earth's orbit from now until Nov.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, KTLA 5

item.title: Oregon woman gets probation after freezing puppies to feed snake, gets to keep pets
item.description: An Oregon woman was sentenced on Wednesday on several animal abuse charges after authorities learned she froze several litters of puppies to feed her pet snakes.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rhode Island woman works 350 hours to build first home
item.description: "It makes you excited to think that you're going to build your own home and help others to get theirs," she told NewsNatoin affiliate WPRI. "That's like a dream come true.
item.dc:creator: Catie McNeil, NewsNation

item.title: Man accused of trying to carjack pregnant woman in Connecticut drive-thru
item.description: With their help, police were able to arrest the suspect, who police say is facing a multitude of charges in this case and others.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Harris campaigns in Wisconsin and Trump in Michigan in battle for 'blue wall' states
item.description: Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris is opening a two-day stay in Wisconsin and Michigan, and Republican candidate Donald Trump will be in Michigan on Thursday as the two candidates grapple for wins in the “blue wall” battleground states, which also include Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports reached a deal Thursday to suspend a three-day strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15 to allow time to negotiate new contract
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports has reached a deal to suspend their strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract, a person briefed on the matter says.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles D.A. to ‘reevaluate’ Menendez brothers’ convictions
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón provided an update Thursday on the convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in August 1989.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9

item.title: Most visited national park site closed indefinitely due to Hurricane Helene
item.description: It comes right as the region – and road – typically sees a boom in usage as weather cools and the leaves change colors.
item.dc:creator: Doug Coats, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Country music star Garth Brooks sued for rape, sexual assault
item.description: LOS ANGELES - Garth Brooks, one of the biggest stars in country music history, has been named in a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist he employed in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Hurricane Kirk strengthens into a Category 4 storm in the Atlantic
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Hurricane Kirk was a Category 4 major hurricane on Thursday, and waves from the system could cause life-threatening surf and rip current conditions this weekend along the U.S. East Coast as well as in Bermuda, the Greater Antilles and the Bahamas, forecasters said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pennsylvania US Senate: Bob Casey, Dave McCormick trade jabs in debate
item.description: The candidates battled throughout the debate, often claiming the other was lying on the campaign trail and on the debate stage.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KRON4

item.title: Doctor who helped supply Matthew Perry ketamine pleads guilty
item.description: A San Diego doctor charged in connection with Matthew Perry’s fatal overdose pleaded guilty Wednesday to conspiring to distribute the surgical anesthetic ketamine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricanes like Helene are deadly when they strike and keep killing for years to come
item.description: Hurricanes in the United States end up hundreds of times deadlier than the government calculates, contributing to more American deaths than car accidents or all the nation's wars, a new study said. The average storm hitting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Port strike may hike prices, cause shortages of certain goods: What to know
item.description: U.S. ports from Maine to Texas shut down Tuesday when the union representing about 45,000 dockworkers went on strike for the first time since 1977.
item.dc:creator: Mae Anderson, Fox 5

item.title: Memorable moments from the vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) clashed Tuesday in what is likely to be the last debate of this presidential election season.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, Fox 5

item.title: After Helene cuts roads, power, phones, Southerners stay in touch the old-fashioned way
item.description: In remote mountain areas, helicopters hoisted the stranded to safety while search crews waded through floodwaters and moved toppled trees so they could look door to door for survivors.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: "So first of all, injured. What does injured mean? Injured means — you mean because they had a headache? Because the bombs never hit the fort," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tennessee plastics factory employee recounts narrow escape through floodwater
item.description: Impact Plastics employee Zinna Adkins stepped outside her workplace at 10:54 a.m. on Friday and captured water rising in the parking lot on her cellphone. But at that time, she said no supervisor informed employees they were free to leave.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, Fox 5

item.title: LIVE UPDATES: Vance, Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees J.D. Vance and Tim Walz are facing off for the first -- and likely only -- time before the 2024 election, which is just over a month away.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC
